quotes: You begin by studying the major market averages to determine the trend of the overall market. Then you select those market sectors or industry groups that are showing the best relative strength. Then you select individual stocks within those groups that are also showing the best relative strength.

concepts.title: Intermarket Principles Applied to Mutual Funds
concepts.body: All technical tools described in intermarket analysis can be applied to charts of mutual funds as a final check on analysis. Price charts and a computer make this work easily achievable across many markets simultaneously.
quotes: all of these technical tools described herein can be applied to charts of mutual funds as a final check on your analysis

concepts.title: Deflation Scenario and Intermarket Relationships
concepts.body: Under deflation, the inverse relationship between bond prices and commodities is maintained (commodities fall, bond prices rise), but the stock market can react negatively — unlike in a disinflationary environment where both bonds and stocks benefit. Deflation is good for bonds but bad for commodities and potentially bad for stocks, causing bonds and stocks to decouple. This is a critical distinction from the normal disinflationary regime where bonds and stocks trend together.
quotes: Disinflation is bad for commodities, but good for bonds and stocks. Deflation is good for bonds and bad for commodities, but may also be bad for stocks.

concepts.title: Bond-Stock Decoupling Under Deflation
concepts.body: During deflationary periods, investors switch out of stocks and into bonds and rate-sensitive stock groups like utilities, causing bonds and stocks to decouple. The 1997–1998 Asian crisis spreading to Russia and Latin America demonstrated this global intermarket linkage, where falling commodity and stock prices boosted Treasury bond prices to record highs.
quotes: For the first time in four years, investors were switching out of stocks and putting more money into bonds and rate-sensitive stock groups like utilities.
Market events of 1998 were a dramatic example of the existence of global intermarket linkages and demonstrated how bonds and stocks can decouple in a deflationary world.

concepts.title: Commodity Exporters and Deflation Risk
concepts.body: A plunge in commodity prices has an especially damaging impact on commodity-exporting countries such as Australia, Canada, Mexico, and Russia. Traders should monitor commodity price trends as a leading indicator of economic stress in these nations and their equity markets.
quotes: A plunge in commodity prices had an especially damaging impact on commodity exporters like Australia, Canada, Mexico, and Russia.

concepts.title: Intermarket Correlation Measurement
concepts.body: Charting software can measure the degree of correlation between different markets. A high positive reading indicates strong positive correlation (markets trend together), a high negative reading indicates strong negative correlation (markets trend opposite), and a reading near zero suggests little or no correlation. Traders should place more weight on intermarket relationships with higher correlations and less weight on those near zero.
quotes: More weight should be placed on those with higher correlations, and less weight on those closer to zero.

concepts.title: Positive vs. Negative Intermarket Correlation
concepts.body: Two markets that normally trend in the same direction, such as bonds and stocks, are positively correlated. Markets that trend in opposite directions, like bonds and commodities, are negatively correlated. Understanding the sign and magnitude of correlation helps traders determine how much emphasis to place on a particular intermarket relationship.
quotes: Two markets that normally trend in the same direction, such as bonds and stocks, are positively correlated. Markets that trend in opposite directions, like bonds and commodities, are negatively correlated.

concepts.title: Moving Average Crossover as Intermarket Filter
concepts.body: A moving-average crossover system in the bond market can be used as a filter for stock index trading. This intermarket filter approach, described by Murray Ruggiero Jr., demonstrates how signals from one market can improve trading system performance in a related market.
quotes: He demonstrates, for example, how a moving-average crossover system in the bond market can be used as a filter for stock index trading.

concepts.title: Relative Strength Ratio Line Breakout
concepts.body: A breakout in the relative strength ratio line of an individual stock versus its sector index signals a potential buy opportunity. Stocks showing a ratio line that is turning up from a base or breaking out to new highs relative to their index are preferred candidates for purchase.

concepts.title: Asset Allocation Using Intermarket Analysis
concepts.body: By incorporating intermarket principles, traders and investors can determine whether the current market climate favors bonds, commodities, or stocks, which directly informs asset allocation decisions. The same framework applies to international investing by comparing relative strength of various global stock markets.
quotes: By incorporating intermarket principles into your decision making process, you can also determine whether the current market climate favors bonds, commodities, or stocks which can play a role in your asset allocation decisions.

concepts.title: Intermarket Ripple Effect
concepts.body: Intermarket analysis recognizes a ripple effect that flows from the dollar to commodities to bonds to stocks. This sequential pattern tracks the business cycle and has implications for economic forecasting. Global linkages mean that events in Asia, Europe, and Latin America impact U.S. markets and vice versa.
quotes: It discusses the ripple effect that flows from the dollar to commodities to bonds to stocks.

concepts.title: Intermarket Sequential Rotation Pattern
concepts.body: The interaction between commodity prices, bond prices, and stock prices follows a sequential pattern that tracks the business cycle. This rotation among the three asset classes can be used in economic forecasting. Dr. Geoffrey Moore documented this in 'Leading Indicators for the 1990s.'
quotes: Dr. Moore substantiates the intermarket rotation within the three asset classes, and argues for their use in economic forecasting.

concepts.title: Relative Strength Analysis for Sector Rotation
concepts.body: Relative strength analysis is used to identify asset classes, market sectors, or individual stocks likely to outperform the general market. It is a key tool within intermarket analysis for sector rotation. This approach helps traders focus capital on the strongest-performing areas of the market.
quotes: Relative strength analysis is helpful for seeking out asset classes, market sectors, or individual stocks that are likely to outperform the general market.

concepts.title: Applying Technical Analysis to Mutual Funds
concepts.body: Technical analysis can be applied to mutual fund charts just as it is applied to any other market, with minor modifications. Mutual fund charts tend to have lower volatility, making them excellent vehicles for chart analysis. All standard technical tools—chart patterns, trendlines, moving averages, oscillators—can be applied to mutual fund charts.
quotes: the lower degree of volatility in mutual fund charts make them excellent vehicles for chart analysis.

concepts.title: Top-Down Approach to Stock Market Analysis
concepts.body: The top-down approach begins with an analysis of the overall market's health, then moves down to market sectors and industry groups, and finally to individual stocks. The goal is to pick the best stocks in the best groups when the stock market is technically healthy. Standard technical tools including chart patterns, volume analysis, trendlines, moving averages, and oscillators are used at each level.
quotes: Your goal is to pick the best stocks in the best groups in an environment when the stock market is technically healthy.

concepts.title: Market Breadth Indicators
concepts.body: Market breadth indicators measure the health of the overall stock market beyond what is reflected in major averages like the Dow. The data used includes advancing versus declining issues, new highs versus new lows, and up volume versus down volume. These internal market readings provide a more accurate picture of overall market health.
quotes: The data used in their construction are advancing versus declining issues, new highs versus new lows, and up volume versus down volume.

concepts.title: Dow Divergence from Broader Market
concepts.body: When the Dow Jones Industrial Average rises but broader averages (S&P 500, Nasdaq, Russell 2000) decline, it signals that the overall market is actually losing ground despite the headline index being up. The broader the average (more stocks included), the more accurately it reflects true market conditions. This divergence is a warning sign that market breadth is negative.
quotes: even though the Dow gained on the day, the overall market lost ground as measured by the more broader based stock averages.

concepts.title: Comparing Major Market Averages for Breadth
concepts.body: Comparing the performance of major stock averages (Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq Composite, Russell 2000) is a method for studying market breadth. When the Dow outperforms broader indices, it suggests internal market weakness. The more stocks included in an average, the more representative it is of the overall market.
quotes: Notice also that the broader the average (the more stocks included) the worse it did.

concepts.title: Advancing vs. Declining Issues as Breadth Signal
concepts.body: When there are more declining stocks than advancing stocks on a given day, market breadth is negative even if the Dow closes higher. Similarly, more declining volume than advancing volume reinforces a negative breadth reading. These figures provide a more accurate picture of market health than the Dow alone.
quotes: there were more declining stocks (1,559) than advancing stocks (1,327), suggesting that the broader market didn't fare as well as the Dow.

concepts.title: New Highs vs. New Lows as Market Health Indicator
concepts.body: The number of stocks hitting new 52-week highs versus new lows is a breadth indicator used to assess the market environment. When new highs exceed new lows, it suggests a positive market environment. This data is reported daily in The Wall Street Journal.
quotes: The number of stocks hitting new 52 week highs (78) was greater than those hitting new lows (43) suggesting a positive market environment.

concepts.title: Closing Tick as Short-Term Market Indicator
concepts.body: The closing tick measures the number of stocks that closed on an uptick versus a downtick. A negative closing tick means more stocks closed on a downtick than an uptick, which is a short-term negative factor. It is reported daily in The Wall Street Journal's Stock Market Data Bank.
quotes: That meant that 135 more stocks closed on a downtick than an uptick, a short term negative factor.

concepts.title: Closing Arms (TRIN) as Breadth Indicator
concepts.body: The closing Arms index (also known as TRIN) is a breadth indicator that can offset other negative signals when it reads at a mildly positive level (e.g., .96). It is reported daily in The Wall Street Journal's Stock Market Data Bank alongside other breadth data. Its interpretation is explained in detail later in the chapter.
quotes: The negative closing tick, however, is offset by a closing Arms (Trin) reading of .96 which is mildly positive.

concepts.title: Advance-Decline (AD) Line Construction
concepts.body: The advance-decline line is constructed by taking the daily difference between the number of advancing issues and declining issues on the NYSE. If advances exceed declines, the daily AD number is positive; if declines exceed advances, it is negative. This daily number is added to a cumulative AD line that displays its own trend.
quotes: The most common way to calculate the AD line is to take the difference between the number of advancing issues and the number of declining issues.

concepts.title: AD Line and Market Average Confirmation Rule
concepts.body: In a healthy market, the advance-decline line and the major market averages should be trending in the same direction. When both the AD line and the Dow are trending upward together, it confirms a healthy bull market. Divergence between the AD line and market averages is a warning signal.
quotes: The idea is to make sure the AD line and the market averages are trending in the same direction.
In a healthy market, both lines should be trending upward together as they are here.

concepts.title: Neural Networks in Intermarket Analysis
concepts.body: Neural networks provide a quantitative framework for identifying and tracking complex relationships among financial markets. They are used to detect hidden patterns and correlations between related markets that are difficult to identify manually. VantagePoint software, introduced in 1991, applies neural network technology to trade interest rate markets, stock indexes, currency markets, and energy futures.
quotes: Neural networks provide a more quantitative framework for identifying and tracking the complex relationships that exist among the financial markets.

concepts.title: Advance-Decline (AD) Line
concepts.body: The AD line measures whether the broader universe of approximately 3500 NYSE stocks is advancing in line with narrower averages like the Dow 30 or S&P 500. It is calculated by subtracting the number of declining issues from the number of advancing issues on a cumulative basis. As long as the AD line advances with the Dow, market breadth is considered healthy. Historically, the AD line peaks well ahead of the major market averages, making it a leading indicator.
quotes: the advance-decline line tells us if the 'troops' are keeping up with the 'generals.'

concepts.title: AD Divergence as a Warning Signal
concepts.body: When the Dow Industrials are hitting new highs while the broader market measured by the AD line is not following, this is called 'bad market breadth' or an AD divergence. This divergence is a warning signal that the market rally may not be sustainable. Technicians watch this closely because the AD line historically peaks well ahead of the major averages.
quotes: The danger appears when the AD line begins to diverge from the Dow.

concepts.title: Daily vs Weekly AD Line
concepts.body: The daily AD line is better suited for short to intermediate comparisons with major stock averages, while the weekly AD line is more useful for trend comparisons spanning several years. A negative divergence in the daily AD line warns of short to intermediate problems, but a similar divergence must also appear in the weekly AD line to confirm a more serious problem is developing.
quotes: it's necessary to also show a similar divergence in the weekly AD line to confirm that a more serious problem is developing.

concepts.title: Advance/Decline Ratio Variation
concepts.body: Because the number of NYSE-listed stocks has grown over time, some analysts prefer dividing the number of advancing issues by the number of declining issues (an advance/decline ratio) rather than subtracting them. This prevents giving greater weight to more recent data due to the larger number of listed stocks. Some analysts also include unchanged issues in the calculation.

concepts.title: McClellan Oscillator
concepts.body: Developed by Sherman McClellan, the McClellan Oscillator is the difference between the 19-day (10% trend) and 39-day (5% trend) exponential moving averages of the daily net advance-decline figures. It fluctuates around a zero line with extremes ranging from +100 to -100. A reading above +100 signals an overbought market; a reading below -100 signals an oversold market. Crossings above and below the zero line are interpreted as short to intermediate term buying and selling signals respectively.

concepts.title: McClellan Oscillator Overbought/Oversold Levels
concepts.body: A McClellan Oscillator reading above +100 is a signal of an overbought stock market, while a reading below -100 is considered an oversold stock market. These extreme readings can be used to identify short to intermediate term market turning points.
quotes: A McClellan Oscillator reading above +100 is a signal of an overbought stock market. A reading below -100 is considered an oversold stock market.

concepts.title: McClellan Oscillator Zero Line Crossings
concepts.body: Crossings of the McClellan Oscillator above the zero line are interpreted as short to intermediate term buying signals, while crossings below the zero line are selling signals. These zero line crossings reflect shifts in the balance of advancing versus declining stocks.
quotes: Crossings above and below the zero line are also interpreted as short to intermediate term buying and selling signals respectively.

concepts.title: McClellan Summation Index
concepts.body: The McClellan Summation Index is a cumulative sum of each day's positive or negative readings in the McClellan Oscillator, making it a longer-range version of the oscillator. It is used to spot major market turning points rather than short to intermediate term signals. Crossings below zero are negative signals, while crossings above zero are positive.
quotes: The Summation Index is used for major trend analysis.

concepts.title: New Highs vs New Lows Indicator
concepts.body: The number of stocks hitting new 52-week highs versus new 52-week lows is a breadth indicator available on a daily and weekly basis. In a strong market, new highs should significantly outnumber new lows. When new highs start to decline or new lows start to expand, a caution signal is given. A 10-day moving average is typically applied to smooth the erratic daily values.
quotes: In a strong market, the number of new highs should be much greater than the number of new lows.

concepts.title: New Lows Crossing Above New Highs as Negative Signal
concepts.body: A negative market signal is activated when the moving average of new lows crosses above the moving average of new highs. Conversely, when new highs reach an extreme, the market has a topping tendency, and when new lows reach an extreme, the market is near a bottom.
quotes: A negative market signal is activated when the moving average of new lows crosses above the moving average of new highs.

concepts.title: New High-New Low Index
concepts.body: The New High-New Low Index plots the difference between the number of stocks hitting new highs and new lows, and can be directly compared to major market averages just like an advance-decline line. A new high in the Dow not matched by a corresponding new high in the high-low line signals weakness in the broader market. Dr. Alexander Elder describes it as 'probably the best leading indicator of the stock market.' Trendline and moving-average analysis can be applied to the line.
quotes: probably the best leading indicator of the stock market

concepts.title: New High-New Low Index Zero Line and Histogram
concepts.body: Elder suggests plotting the New High-New Low Index as a histogram with a horizontal reference at the zero line to make divergences easier to spot. Crossings above and below the zero line reflect bullish and bearish shifts in market psychology respectively.
quotes: crossings above and below the zero line also reflect bullish and bearish shifts in market psychology.

concepts.title: Upside vs Downside Volume
concepts.body: The NYSE provides volume data for both advancing and declining issues, allowing comparison of upside versus downside volume to measure which is dominant. When upside volume is dominant, the market is strong; when downside volume is greater, the market is weak. The data can be shown as two separate lines or as a single difference line, and a 10-day moving average is typically applied.
quotes: When the upside volume is dominant, the market is strong. When downside volume is greater, the market is weak.

concepts.title: Arms Index (TRIN)
concepts.body: The Arms Index, created by Richard Arms, is a ratio of ratios: the numerator is advancing issues divided by declining issues, and the denominator is advancing volume divided by declining volume. A reading below 1.0 indicates more volume in rising stocks and is positive; a reading above 1.0 reflects more volume in declining issues and is negative. It is a contrary indicator that trends in the opposite direction of the market.
quotes: A reading below 1.0 indicates more volume in rising stocks and is positive. A reading above 1.0 reflects more volume in declining issues and is negative.

concepts.title: Arms Index Intraday Interpretation
concepts.body: On an intraday basis, a very high Arms Index reading is positive (contrary signal indicating oversold conditions), while a very low reading is negative (indicating overbought conditions). The Arms Index can be used for intraday trading by tracking its direction and spotting signs of short-term market extremes.
quotes: On an intraday basis, a very high Arms Index reading is positive, while a very low reading is negative.

concepts.title: TICK Indicator
concepts.body: The TICK indicator measures the difference between the number of stocks trading on an uptick versus the number trading on a downtick. It is a minute-by-minute version of the daily advance-decline line and is used for the same purpose of gauging market breadth on an intraday basis.
quotes: The TICK is a minute-by-minute version of the daily advance-decline line

concepts.title: Combining TRIN and TICK for Intraday Trading
concepts.body: The Arms Index (TRIN) and TICK indicator can be used together for intraday trading. A rising TICK combined with a falling TRIN is a positive signal, while a falling TICK combined with a rising TRIN is a negative signal. This combination provides a more complete picture of intraday market conditions.
quotes: a rising TICK indicator and a falling Arms Index (TRIN) are positive, while a falling TICK indicator and a rising Arms Index (TRIN) are negative.

concepts.title: AD Line Breadth Confirmation Rule
concepts.body: The primary use of the advance-decline line in all its forms is to measure the direction of the broader market and ensure it is moving in the same direction as the more narrowly constructed but popular market averages. Divergence between the two is the key warning signal regardless of the specific calculation method used.
quotes: its use is always the same—that is, to measure the direction of the broader market and to ensure it's moving in the same direction as the more narrowly constructed, but popular market averages.

concepts.title: Arms Index (TRIN) Overview
concepts.body: The Arms Index (also called TRIN) trends in the opposite direction of the market, making it a contrary indicator. Exceptionally high spikes in the Arms Index usually signal market bottoms. It is quoted throughout the trading day and has short-term forecasting value.
quotes: The Arms Index (also called TRIN) trends in the opposite direction of the market. Exceptionally high spikes usually signal market bottoms.

concepts.title: 10-Day Moving Average of Arms Index – Oversold/Overbought Levels
concepts.body: Most traders use a 10-day moving average of the Arms Index rather than its raw daily value. A 10-day average above 1.20 is considered oversold (bullish signal), while a value below 0.70 is considered overbought (bearish signal). These threshold numbers may shift depending on the overall trend of the market.
quotes: a 10 day average of the Arms Index above 1.20 is considered oversold, while a 10 day Arms value below .70 is overbought, although those numbers may shift depending on the overall trend of the market

concepts.title: Fibonacci Numbers Applied to Arms Index
concepts.body: Richard Arms himself expresses a preference for Fibonacci numbers when applying moving averages to the Arms Index. He suggests using a 21-day Arms Index in addition to the standard 10-day version. He also utilizes 21-day and 55-day moving-average crossovers of the Arms Index to generate good intermediate-term trades.
quotes: He suggests using a 21 day Arms Index in addition to the 10 day version. He also utilizes 21 day and 55 day moving-average crossovers of the Arms Index to generate good intermediate term trades.

concepts.title: 21-Day and 55-Day Arms Index Crossover Signal
concepts.body: Using 21-day and 55-day moving-average crossovers of the Arms Index can generate good intermediate-term trade signals. This approach applies Fibonacci-based periods to the contrary indicator to identify turning points in the broader market trend.
quotes: He also utilizes 21 day and 55 day moving-average crossovers of the Arms Index to generate good intermediate term trades.

concepts.title: Open Arms Index
concepts.body: The Open Arms Index is an alternative calculation of the Arms Index where each of the four components in the formula is averaged separately over a period of 10 days, and then the Arms Index is calculated from those four different averages. This produces a much smoother version of the indicator compared to the standard method of smoothing the final daily value. Many analysts prefer the Open Arms version, and different moving average lengths such as 21 and 55 days can also be applied to it.
quotes: In the 'Open' version of the Arms Index, each of the four components in the formula is averaged separately over a period of 10 days.
The 10 day Open Arms Index gives a much smoother look to this indicator, but still trends in the opposite direction of the market.

concepts.title: Open Arms Index Moving Average Crossover Signal
concepts.body: A crossing of the Open Arms Index's 10-day moving average often signals turning points in the market. Because the Open Arms version is smoother, its crossover signals may be more reliable and less prone to whipsaws than the raw Arms Index.
quotes: A crossing of its 10-day moving average (darker line) often signals turning points.

concepts.title: Equivolume Charting
concepts.body: Equivolume charting is a form of charting pioneered by Richard Arms that combines price and volume analysis into a single visual element. Each price bar is shown as a rectangle where the height measures the day's trading range and the width is determined by that day's volume. Heavier volume days produce wider rectangles, while lighter volume days produce narrower rectangles, allowing traders to assess the relationship between price movement and volume at a glance.
quotes: On the Equivolume chart, each price bar is shown as a rectangle. The height of the rectangle measures the day's trading range. The width of the rectangle is determined by that day's volume.

concepts.title: Equivolume – Widening Rectangles as Bearish Signal
concepts.body: When rectangles on an Equivolume chart start to widen during a sell-off, it indicates that volume is increasing on down moves, which is a negative sign for the market or stock. This widening during a decline suggests distribution and confirms the bearish price action with heavy volume.
quotes: The rectangles started to widen during Intel's last sell-off—a negative sign.

concepts.title: Equivolume Chart Bullish Breakout Rule
concepts.body: A bullish price breakout should always be accompanied by a burst of trading activity. On an Equivolume chart, this is represented by a noticeably wider rectangle. In an uptrend, up days should show wider rectangles while down days should show narrower rectangles.
quotes: a bullish price breakout should always be accompanied by a burst of trading activity

concepts.title: Equivolume Charting
concepts.body: Equivolume charting combines price and volume analysis into one chart by representing each trading period as a rectangle whose width is determined by volume. It makes comparisons between price and volume much easier and can be applied to market averages as well as individual stocks on both daily and weekly charts.

concepts.title: CandlePower (Candlevolume) Charting
concepts.body: CandlePower charting, proposed by Greg Morris, combines candlestick charts with Equivolume charting so that the width of each candlestick is determined by volume — the greater the volume, the wider the candlestick. This hybrid technique is also called Candlevolume and is available in Metastock software.
quotes: the CandlePower chart offers similar if not better information than Equivolume or candlestick charting and is as visually appealing as either of them

concepts.title: Confirming Dow Breakouts with S&P 500 and NYSE Composite
concepts.body: Breakouts in the Dow Industrials should be confirmed by similar breakouts in both the S&P 500 and the NYSE Composite Index for the breakout to have staying power. This multi-index confirmation reduces the risk of acting on false breakouts.
quotes: Breakouts in the Dow Industrials should, as a rule, be confirmed by similar breakouts in both the S&P 500 and the NYSE Composite Index if the breakout is to have staying power

concepts.title: Nasdaq as Technology Sector Proxy
concepts.body: The Nasdaq Composite, though containing the largest number of stocks, is capitalization-weighted and dominated by the largest technology stocks, making it more a measure of the technology sector's direction than the broader market. The Russell 2000 is a truer measure of the smaller stock universe.
quotes: the Nasdaq is more often a measure of the direction of the technology sector

concepts.title: Nasdaq/S&P 500 Ratio for Technology Leadership
concepts.body: A ratio of the Nasdaq to the S&P 500 reveals whether technology stocks are leading or lagging the broader market. A rising ratio line indicates technology leadership, which is generally a positive sign for the overall market.
quotes: It's usually better for the market if they're leading and the ratio line is rising

concepts.title: Russell 2000/S&P 500 Ratio for Market Breadth
concepts.body: Comparing the Russell 2000 to the S&P 500 reveals whether small-cap 'troops' are following large-cap 'generals.' When small stocks show poor relative strength or lag too far behind large stocks, it is often a warning that market breadth is weakening.
quotes: When the small stocks are showing poor relative strength, or are lagging too far behind the large stocks, that's often a warning that market breadth is weakening

concepts.title: Multiple Market Averages Trending Together
concepts.body: The greater the number of stock market averages trending in the same direction, the greater the chances are for that trend to continue. Analysts should also check the advance-decline line, new highs-new lows line, and upside-downside volume lines to confirm trend direction.
quotes: the greater the number of stock market averages that are trending in the same direction, the greater the chances are for that trend continuing

concepts.title: Dow Theory Confirmation Rule
concepts.body: A Dow Theory buy signal is present when both the Dow Industrials and the Dow Transports hit new highs simultaneously. When one diverges from the other, a caution signal is given.
quotes: A Dow Theory buy signal is present when both averages hit new highs. When one diverges from the other, a caution signal is given

concepts.title: Technical Analysis Checklist — Market Analysis Phase
concepts.body: Before forming a market conclusion, a trader should systematically evaluate: overall market direction, sector directions, weekly and monthly charts, major/intermediate/minor trend directions, support and resistance levels, trendlines and channels, volume and open interest confirmation, retracement levels (33%, 50%, 66%), price gaps, reversal and continuation patterns, price objectives, moving average direction, oscillator overbought/oversold status, oscillator divergences, contrary opinion extremes, Elliott Wave patterns, Fibonacci retracements/projections, cycle tops/bottoms, market translation, computer trend direction, and point-and-figure or candlestick signals.

concepts.title: Technical Analysis Checklist — Trade Execution Phase
concepts.body: After forming a bullish or bearish conclusion, a trader must answer: which way will the market trend over the next several months, whether to buy or sell, how many units to trade, how much risk is acceptable if wrong, what the profit objective is, where to enter the market, what type of order to use, and where to place the protective stop.

concepts.title: Keys to Successful Trading
concepts.body: The keys to successful trading are knowledge, discipline, and patience. Discipline and patience are best achieved by doing homework and having a plan of action before entering the market. Going through a checklist won't guarantee right conclusions but helps ensure the right questions are asked.
quotes: The keys to successful trading are knowledge, discipline, and patience
Asking the right questions is the surest way of finding the right answers

concepts.title: Technical Factors Lead Known Fundamentals
concepts.body: Technical factors tend to lead the known fundamentals, meaning price charts often signal important market moves before the fundamental reasons become apparent. Any important market move must ultimately be caused by underlying fundamental factors, so technicians benefit from having some awareness of fundamental conditions.
quotes: technical factors do lead the known fundamentals
any important market move must be caused by underlying fundamental factors

concepts.title: Market Reaction to Fundamental News as Technical Signal
concepts.body: Observing how the market reacts to fundamental news can serve as an excellent technical indication. Unexpected or counterintuitive reactions to news often reveal the underlying trend's true strength or weakness.
quotes: seeing how the market reacts to fundamental news can be used as an excellent technical indication

concepts.title: Using Technical Analysis as a Trend Filter for Fundamentalists
concepts.body: Fundamental analysts can use price charts or computer trend-following systems as a filter to prevent taking positions opposite an existing trend. Unusual chart action can also alert the fundamental analyst to examine the fundamental situation more closely.
quotes: The fundamentalist can consult a price chart or use a computer trend-following system as a filter to prevent him or her from assuming a position opposite an existing trend

concepts.title: Combining Multiple Technical Techniques
concepts.body: Technical analysis is a blend of many approaches, each adding to the analyst's knowledge. The key is knowing which tools to emphasize in the current market situation, as each works better in certain conditions. The more factors an analyst considers, the better the chances of finding the right clue to future market movement.
quotes: My approach to market analysis is to combine as many techniques as possible
The more factors the analyst considers, the better the chances of finding that right clue

concepts.title: Chartered Market Technician (CMT) Designation
concepts.body: The CMT is a three-step examination process instituted by the Market Technicians Association (MTA) that qualifies analysts to carry the CMT designation. It serves as a professional credential for technical analysts, analogous to the CFA for security analysts or the bar exam for lawyers. Traders and investors should verify that their technical analyst holds a CMT.
quotes: The next time someone offers you his or her technical opinion, ask to see the CMT.

concepts.title: Demand Index (DI) — Definition and Construction
concepts.body: The Demand Index (DI) is an early volume indicator developed in the 1970s by James Sibbett. It is the ratio of buying pressure to selling pressure, incorporating both price and volume. When buying pressure exceeds selling pressure, the DI is above zero (positive/bullish); when selling pressure dominates, the DI is below zero (negative/bearish).
quotes: The Demand Index is the ratio of buying pressure to selling pressure.

concepts.title: Demand Index (DI) — Zero Line Signal
concepts.body: A DI reading above the zero line indicates greater buying pressure and is considered positive/bullish for prices. A DI reading below the zero line indicates greater selling pressure and implies prices will move lower. Crossings of the zero line can serve as confirmation signals for trend changes.
quotes: When the buying pressure is greater than the selling pressure, the DI is above the zero line, which is positive. Greater selling pressure means the DI is below zero, which implies prices will move lower.

concepts.title: Demand Index (DI) — Bullish Divergence Signal
concepts.body: A bullish (positive) divergence occurs when prices make lower lows while the DI forms higher lows. This pattern suggests that selling pressure is diminishing and that prices may be bottoming. The divergence is confirmed when the DI subsequently crosses above the zero line.
quotes: While prices made lower lows (line A), the DI formed higher lows (line B). This is a classic positive, or bullish divergence, which suggested that bond prices were bottoming. The divergence was confirmed when the DI moved above the zero line at point 1.

concepts.title: Demand Index (DI) — Bearish Divergence Signal
concepts.body: A bearish (negative) divergence occurs when prices make higher highs while the DI forms lower highs. This pattern warns of a potential price peak and impending decline. In the T-Bond example, the DI formed a significantly lower high while the bond contract was nearly six points higher, warning of a price peak.
quotes: While the DI was lower (line D), the bond contract was almost six points higher (line C). This negative or bearish divergence warned of a price peak.

concepts.title: Demand Index (DI) — Trendline Analysis
concepts.body: The DI can be plotted as a line (rather than a histogram) to facilitate trendline analysis on the indicator itself. Trendline breaks on the DI often precede trendline breaks on the price chart, providing an early warning signal. Buying on a DI trendline break can significantly improve entry price compared to waiting for the price trendline break.
quotes: I have personally found trendline analysis of indicators to be quite valuable. Indicator trendlines are often broken ahead of price trendlines.

concepts.title: Indicator Trendline Breaks as Early Entry Signals
concepts.body: When an indicator such as the DI breaks its downtrend line before the corresponding price downtrend is broken, this provides an early entry signal. Acting on the indicator trendline break rather than waiting for the price trendline break can meaningfully improve the entry price. This principle applies broadly to trendline analysis of technical indicators.
quotes: As this chart indicates, buying just one week earlier could have significantly improved the entry price.

concepts.title: Demand Index (DI) — Divergence as Advance Warning of Price Decline
concepts.body: When the DI forms lower highs while price makes new highs, this bearish divergence can provide advance warning of a serious price decline well before the decline actually occurs. In the GM example, the DI warning came well ahead of a serious price decline in June and July, giving traders time to act.
quotes: This warning signal came well ahead of the serious price decline in June and July.

concepts.title: Technical Analysis Validated by Federal Reserve Research
concepts.body: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York published a 1995 Staff Report titled 'Head and Shoulders: Not Just a Flaky Pattern,' which found that technical analysis generates statistically significant profits despite being incompatible with the Efficient Market Hypothesis. A 1997 Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis report further supported the failure of the simple efficient market hypothesis to describe how foreign exchange markets actually function.
quotes: Technical analysis, the prediction of price movements based on past price movements, has been shown to generate statistically significant profits despite its incompatibility with most economists' notions of 'efficient markets.'

The success of technical trading rules shown in the previous section is typical of a number of later studies showing that the simple efficient market hypothesis fails in important ways to describe how the foreign exchange market actually functions.

concepts.title: Intermarket Analysis as Evolution of Technical Analysis
concepts.body: The introduction of intermarket analysis has shifted the focus of technical analysis from single-market analysis to a more interdependent view of financial markets. All global markets are considered linked, making technical analysis a universal language especially useful in an increasingly intertwined global financial system.
quotes: The introduction of intermarket analysis, for example, has changed the focus away from 'single market' analysis to a more interdependent view of the financial markets.

concepts.title: Independent Testing Requirement for Technical Methods
concepts.body: Before applying any technical approach in actual investing, it is always recommended that investors conduct their own independent testing and research. This applies to advanced technical indicators as well as standard methods.
quotes: As with any technical approach, it is always recommended that investors do their own independent testing and research before actually investing.

concepts.title: Volume Analysis as Key Ingredient in Market Direction
concepts.body: Most technicians agree that volume analysis is an important ingredient in determining a market's direction. Indicators like the Demand Index incorporate both price and volume to provide a more complete picture of market dynamics than price alone.
quotes: Most technicians will agree that volume analysis is an important ingredient in determining a market's direction.

concepts.title: Herrick Payoff Index (HPI) – Overview
concepts.body: The Herrick Payoff Index was developed by John Herrick to analyze commodity futures through changes in open interest. It uses price, volume, and open interest to determine money flow into or out of a given commodity. It helps traders spot divergences between price action and open interest, and can identify buying or selling panics.
quotes: The Herrick Payoff Index uses price, volume, and open interest to determine money flow into or out of a given commodity.

concepts.title: HPI Zero Line – Basic Interpretation
concepts.body: The most basic interpretation of the HPI is whether it is above or below the zero line. A positive value (above zero) means the HPI is projecting higher prices and that open interest is rising along with prices. Negative readings suggest that funds are flowing out of the commodity being analyzed.
quotes: A positive value means that the HPI is projecting higher prices and that open interest is rising along with prices.
negative readings suggest that funds are flowing out of the commodity being analyzed.

concepts.title: HPI Zero Line Crossings – Buy and Sell Signals
concepts.body: Crossings of the HPI above the zero line are interpreted as buy signals, while crossings below the zero line are interpreted as sell signals. On daily data, the HPI may cross above and below the zero line several times before a longer-lasting buy or sell signal is confirmed. This characteristic means daily signals can produce multiple false or short-term signals before a sustained trend signal emerges.
quotes: Crossings above zero are buys (B); crossings below are sells (S).
This is characteristic of the HPI when used on the daily data as it will cross above and below the zero line several times before a longer lasting buy or sell signal is given.

concepts.title: HPI – Weekly Data Preferred for Fewer False Signals
concepts.body: The HPI, like the Demand Index, is most effective when used on weekly data, as fewer false signals are evident compared to daily data. Traders should prefer weekly charts when using the HPI to filter out noise and short-term whipsaws.
quotes: The HPI, like the Demand Index, is most effective when used on the weekly data, as fewer false signals are evident.

concepts.title: HPI Divergence Analysis – Warning of Trend Change
concepts.body: Divergence analysis using the HPI can warn traders of a change from positive to negative money flow before it is reflected in price. A negative divergence occurs when prices make a higher high while the HPI forms a lower high, signaling weakening money flow and a potential price decline. This was demonstrated in T-Bond futures where the HPI peaked in early 1993 while bonds were nearly 10 points higher, warning of the 1994 price decline.
quotes: Divergence analysis can also be used to warn the trader of a change from positive to negative money flow.
the HPI was forming a lower high (line B). This negative divergence warned bond traders of the decline in prices that took place in 1994.

concepts.title: HPI and Open Interest – Trend Confirmation
concepts.body: Changes in open interest provide important clues as to whether a market trend is well supported. The HPI incorporates open interest data to help determine whether money is flowing into or out of a commodity, thereby confirming or questioning the sustainability of a price trend. Rising open interest alongside rising prices (positive HPI) confirms bullish trend support.
quotes: changes in the open interest can give traders important clues as to whether a market trend is well supported or not.

concepts.title: HPI – Identification of Buying and Selling Panics
concepts.body: The HPI is particularly useful for identifying buying or selling panics in commodity markets through analysis of open interest behavior. Extreme or rapid changes in the HPI relative to price can signal panic-driven market moves, giving traders an early warning of potential reversals or exhaustion points.
quotes: buying or selling panics can often be identified through analysis of the open interest by the Herrick Payoff Index.

concepts.title: HPI – Application Specific to Futures Markets
concepts.body: The HPI is designed specifically for use in futures markets because it incorporates open interest, which is a data series unique to futures and options markets. It is not applicable to equity markets where open interest data is not available in the same form.
quotes: HPI uses price, volume, and open interest in its calculation and is used in futures markets.

concepts.title: Herrick Payoff Index (HPI) Divergence Signals
concepts.body: The HPI incorporates open interest analysis to help determine a commodity market's direction. Bearish divergences occur when prices make higher highs while HPI makes lower highs; bullish divergences occur when prices make lower lows while HPI forms higher lows. A cross of the HPI zero line from below is a bullish signal, and from above is bearish. The HPI bottoming well ahead of prices (positive divergence) signals an impending price reversal upward.
quotes: These two examples should illustrate why the HPI and its analysis of open interest can be helpful in analyzing a commodity market's direction.

concepts.title: STARC Bands Construction
concepts.body: STARC Bands (Stoller Average Range Channels) are constructed by doubling the 15-period Average True Range and adding/subtracting it from a 6-period moving average. The upper band is starc+ and the lower band is starc-. Movement outside these bands is uncommon and indicates an extreme situation.
quotes: In his formula the 15 period Average True Range is doubled and added to or subtracted from a 6 period moving average (MA).
Movement outside of these bands is uncommon and indicates an extreme situation.

concepts.title: STARC Bands as Trading Filters — High/Low Risk Zones
concepts.body: When prices are near or above the starc+ band, it is a high-risk time to buy and a low-risk time to sell. When prices are at or below the starc- band, it is a high-risk selling zone and a more favorable point to buy. These bands help traders avoid chasing the market, which almost always results in a poor entry price.
quotes: When prices are near or above the starc+ band, it is a high risk time to buy and a low risk time to sell.
Conversely, if prices are at or below the starc- band, then it is a high risk selling zone and a more favorable point to buy.
Starc bands can help the trader avoid chasing the market, which almost always results in a poor entry price.

concepts.title: STARC Bands — Sell Signal at Upper Band
concepts.body: When prices rise to or above the starc+ band, this represents a low-risk selling opportunity. In the gold futures example, prices reaching the starc+ band (point 2) provided a low-risk sell entry after having been near the starc- band just three weeks earlier.
quotes: Point 2 was a low risk selling opportunity.

concepts.title: STARC Bands — Buy Signal at Lower Band
concepts.body: When prices dip to or below the starc- band, this represents a low-risk buying opportunity and a high-risk time to sell. In the gold example, prices touching the starc- band multiple times (points 1, 3, 4) led to stabilization or higher prices within 1–2 weeks.

concepts.title: STARC Bands — Multi-Timeframe Applicability
concepts.body: STARC bands work well in all time frames, even as short as 5 to 10 minute bar charts, making them versatile tools for both short-term and longer-term traders.
quotes: These bands work well in all time frames even as short as 5 to 10 minute bar charts.

concepts.title: Keltner Channels Construction
concepts.body: Keltner Channels use a 10-period Average True Range (ATR) doubled and added to/subtracted from a 20-period exponential moving average (EMA). The plus band equals the 20-period EMA plus 2×ATR(10), and the minus band equals the 20-period EMA minus 2×ATR(10). This modification was popularized by Linda Raschke.
quotes: the ATR is calculated over 10 periods. This ATR value is then doubled and added to a 20 period exponential moving average for the plus band and subtracted from it for the minus band.

concepts.title: Keltner Channels — Breakout Interpretation (Bullish)
concepts.body: When prices close above the Keltner plus band, a positive signal is generated indicating a breakout in upward volatility and that prices will continue higher. This is analogous to a four-week channel breakout system.
quotes: When prices close above the plus band, a positive signal is given as it indicates a breakout in upward volatility.

concepts.title: Keltner Channels — Breakout Interpretation (Bearish)
concepts.body: When prices close below the Keltner minus band, a negative signal is generated indicating prices will move lower and a new downtrend may begin. The negative signal remains in effect until prices close above the plus band.
quotes: when prices close below the lower band, it is negative and indicates prices will move lower.
Until prices close above the plus band, the negative signal will stay in effect.

concepts.title: Keltner Channels — EMA as Support in Rising Market
concepts.body: In a rising market following a Keltner breakout above the plus band, the 20-period EMA should act as support on pullbacks. A retest of the EMA after a bullish breakout can serve as a secondary entry point before the next leg higher.
quotes: In a rising market the 20 period EMA should act as support.

concepts.title: Keltner Channels vs. STARC Bands — Contrasting Use
concepts.body: STARC bands are used as mean-reversion filters (prices at extremes signal high-risk continuation and low-risk reversal), while Keltner channels are used as breakout signals (prices closing outside the band signal continuation in the breakout direction). Despite both being ATR-based, their trading interpretations are opposite.
quotes: The recommended use of the Keltner channels is much different from the starc bands.

concepts.title: ATR-Based Bands as Supplementary Tools
concepts.body: Neither STARC bands nor Keltner channels are presented as stand-alone trading systems; they should be considered additional tools to complement other analysis methods such as percentage envelopes or Bollinger Bands.
quotes: Neither is presented as a stand-alone trading system but should be considered as additional tools of the trade.

concepts.title: Average True Range (ATR) Definition
concepts.body: Average True Range is the average of true price ranges over x periods. True Range is the greatest of: today's high to low, yesterday's close to today's high, or yesterday's close to today's low. It was developed by Welles Wilder.
quotes: True Range is the greatest distance from today's high to low, yesterday's close to today's high, or yesterday's close to today's low.

concepts.title: Demand Index (DI) Formula
concepts.body: The Demand Index calculates Buying Pressure (BP) and Selling Pressure (SP) and takes their ratio (DI = BP/SP). When prices rise, BP = Volume and SP = V/P; when prices decline, BP = V/P and SP = Volume, where P is the percentage change in price modified by constant K = (3×C)/VA, with VA being the 10-day average of a two-day price range. If BP > SP, then DI = SP/BP.
quotes: DI is BP/SP.
K=(3xC/VA) Where C is the closing price and VA (Volatility Average) is the 10 day average of a two day price range (highest high - lowest low).

concepts.title: Market Profile — Statistical Price Distribution Concept
concepts.body: Market Profile is a statistical approach to price analysis that displays price as a frequency distribution histogram turned on its side, forming a bell-shaped normal distribution. The modal price represents the most frequently traded price, and approximately 68.3% of prices fall within one standard deviation of the mean, while virtually all values fall within three standard deviations.
quotes: Market Profile is essentially a statistical approach to the analysis of price data.
virtually all values fall within three (3) standard deviations of the average while about 70% (68.3% to be exact) fall within one (1) standard deviation of the average

concepts.title: Market Profile — Equilibrium vs. Disequilibrium
concepts.body: Market Profile portrays equilibrium as periods of market balance when price distributions are symmetric, and disequilibrium as periods of market imbalance when price distributions are skewed or not symmetric. The market is always either in equilibrium or moving toward it, reflecting the balance between buyers and sellers.
quotes: the market is either in equilibrium or moving toward it.

the profile graphic portrays equilibrium as periods of market balance—when price distributions are symmetric, and represents disequilibrium as periods of market imbalance—when price distributions are not symmetric or are skewed.

concepts.title: Market Profile as Decision Support Tool
concepts.body: Market Profile is not a trading system and does not provide trade recommendations. Its aim is to allow the user to witness a market's developing value on price reoccurrence over time. It is a decision support tool requiring the user to exercise personal judgment in the trading process.
quotes: Market Profile is not a trading system nor does it provide trade recommendations. The aim of the profile graphic is to allow the user to witness a market's developing value on price reoccurrence over time.

concepts.title: Market Profile Graphic Construction
concepts.body: The Market Profile format organizes price and time into a visual representation of a single session. It is constructed by assigning a letter to each price within each period's price range (A for the 1st period, B for the 2nd, etc.) and then collapsing each price range into the leftmost column. The completed profile reflects prices on the left and period frequency of price occurrence on the right.

concepts.title: Time Price Opportunity (TPO)
concepts.body: Each letter in a Market Profile graphic represents a Time Price Opportunity (TPO), identifying a specific price at which the market traded during a specific time period. TPOs are the basic units of analysis for the day's activity. Each TPO is an opportunity created by the market at a certain time and certain price, and Market Profile distributions are constructed of TPOs.
quotes: each TPO is an opportunity created by the market at a certain time and certain price

concepts.title: CBOT Half-Hour Letter Assignment
concepts.body: The Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) assigns a letter to each half-hour trading period on a 24-hour basis. Uppercase letters A through X represent the half-hour periods from midnight to noon, while lowercase letters from a through x represent the half-hour periods from noon to midnight. Letter assignments can vary between vendors.

concepts.title: Market Auction Setting Principle
concepts.body: The purpose of the marketplace is to facilitate or promote trade, and all market activity occurs within an auction setting. As price moves higher, more buying comes in; as price moves lower, more selling comes in. The market moves up to shut off buying (auctioning up until the last buyer buys) and moves down to shut off selling (auctioning down until the last seller sells), operating through a dual auction process.
quotes: the market continues to auction up, advertising for sellers to appear in order to shut off buying

concepts.title: Dual Auction Process
concepts.body: The market operates in a dual auction mode where prices rotate around a fair or mean price area. When price moves up and more buying comes in, the up-move advertises for an opposite response (selling) to stop the directional move. The opposite is true when price moves down, creating a self-regulating auction mechanism.

concepts.title: Continuous Negotiation and Range Parameters
concepts.body: When a market moves directionally it establishes range parameters — an unfair high and an unfair low — and then trades between them to establish a fair value area. All trade takes place through this negotiating process and remains within these parameters until one side is eventually taken out, forming a new high or new low.

concepts.title: Market Balance and Imbalance
concepts.body: The market is either in equilibrium or working toward equilibrium between buyers and sellers. To facilitate trade, the market moves from a state of balance (equilibrium) to one of imbalance (disequilibrium) and back to balance again. This pattern of market behavior occurs at all time frames, from intraday to longer-term consolidated sessions.

concepts.title: Short Term vs. Longer Term Trader Time Frames
concepts.body: Market activity is divided into two timeframe categories: short term (day timeframe — locals, day traders, options traders on expiration) who are forced to trade today and seek a fair price, and longer term (commercials, swing traders, position traders) who are not forced to trade today and seek an advantageous price. Longer term buyers seek lower prices while longer term sellers seek higher prices, so they generally do not trade with each other at the same price and time.
quotes: the short term trader is seeking a fair price
Not forced to trade today and with time as an ally, these traders can seek a more advantageous price

concepts.title: Initial Balance Established in First Hour
concepts.body: A market's initial balance — a place where two-sided trade can occur — is usually established in the first hour of trade by short term buyers and sellers (day timeframe activity) in their pursuit of a fair price. Most of the day's activity occurs in the fair price or value area. Prices above and below this developed fair value area offer opportunity and are advantageous to longer term traders.

concepts.title: Role of Longer Term Trader in Range Development
concepts.body: Longer term traders, by entering the market with large enough volume, can upset the initial balance and extend the price range higher or lower. The longer term trader is responsible for the way the day's range develops and for the duration of the longer term auction. In other words, the role of the longer term trader is to move the market directionally.
quotes: the role of the longer term trader is to move the market directionally

concepts.title: Price vs. Value Distinction
concepts.body: There are two kinds of prices: those that are accepted (a price area where the market trades over time) and those that are rejected (a price area where the market spends very little time). A rejected price is considered excessive — an unfair high or unfair low. Price is observable and objective while value is perceived and subjective, depending upon the particular needs of longer term traders.
quotes: Price is observable and objective while value is perceived and subjective

concepts.title: Responsive vs. Initiating Longer Term Activity
concepts.body: When a longer term trader responds to an advertised price in the expected manner (buying after prices fall, selling after prices rise), this behavior is referred to as responsive. If the longer term trader does the opposite (buys after prices rose or sells after prices declined), this unexpected activity is referred to as initiating. Classifying longer term activity as responsive or initiating relative to yesterday's or today's evolving value area provides anecdotal evidence of longer term trader confidence — the more confident the trader, the more likely he is to take initiating action.
quotes: The more confident the trader becomes, the more likely he is to take initiating action

concepts.title: Normal Day Pattern
concepts.body: A normal day occurs when the longer term trader is relatively inactive. The day's range is established in the pioneer range (the first column of prices) during the session's first half-hour period of trade. The short term trader establishes the initial balance, the unfair high and low, and then prices rotate between these parameters for the balance of the day.

concepts.title: Normal Variation Day Pattern
concepts.body: A normal variation day occurs when the longer term trader is more active and extends the range beyond the initial balance. The short term trader's initial balance parameters do not hold and there is some directional movement which extends the range and sets a new high or new low parameter. As a rule, the range extension beyond the initial balance can be anywhere from a couple of ticks to double the initial balance, and this profile type is probably the most common.
quotes: the range extension beyond the initial balance can be anywhere from a couple of ticks to double the initial balance

concepts.title: Combining Daily Profiles into Cumulative Profile
concepts.body: By combining daily consecutive profile graphics into a larger cumulative profile graphic, an evolving picture of long-term balance or imbalance emerges. This aggregated view allows traders to assess the longer-term auction context beyond a single session.

concepts.title: Trend Day Definition
concepts.body: A trend day occurs when the longer term trader extends the range successively further beyond the initial balance, with the range being considerably more than double the initial balance. The longer term trader controls direction as the market continues its search for a fair price. The market moves in one direction and closes at or near the directional extreme.
quotes: A trend day occurs when the longer term trader extends the range successively further. In this instance, the range is considerably more than double the initial balance with the longer term trader controlling direction as the market continues its search for a fair price.

concepts.title: Neutral Day Definition
concepts.body: A neutral day occurs when the longer term trader extends the range after the initial balance in one direction, then reverses and extends the range in the opposite direction. Neutral days indicate trader uncertainty and occur when the market probes or tests for price trend continuation or change.
quotes: A neutral day occurs when the longer term trader extends the range after the initial balance in one direction, then reverses and extends the range in the opposite direction. Neutral days indicate trader uncertainty and occur when the market probes or tests for price trend continuation or change.

concepts.title: Normal Day Characteristics
concepts.body: A normal day features an initial balance established by short term traders in the first two periods, with longer term traders remaining inactive. The result is a symmetrical or balanced price distribution. The range does not extend significantly beyond the initial balance.

concepts.title: Normal Variation Day Characteristics
concepts.body: A normal variation day features an initial balance established by short term traders, after which longer term traders extend the range to almost double the initial balance. This distinguishes it from a full trend day where the range exceeds double the initial balance.

concepts.title: Longer Term Trader as Directional Driver
concepts.body: The longer term trader is responsible for determining the market's directional movement. Monitoring longer term trader activity helps detect evidence of a price trend. After identifying and evaluating longer term trader activity, an educated conclusion regarding price direction can be reached.
quotes: Because the longer term trader is responsible for determining the market's directional movement, we monitor this activity to help detect evidence of a price trend.

concepts.title: Tracking Longer Term Trader Influence in Daily Range
concepts.body: The profile graphic helps identify longer term trader behavior during daily range development. By monitoring longer term activity throughout the range — particularly at the extremes, at range extension, and after value area completion — traders can determine whether longer term buyers or sellers are more active and hence control market direction.
quotes: Activity at the extremes provides the clearest indication of longer term trader influence, followed by range extension and then value area buying and selling.

concepts.title: Extremes Formation Rule — Minimum Two Single Prints
concepts.body: Extremes are formed when the longer term trader competes with the short term trader for opportunities at a particular price level, which later becomes either the session high or low. A minimum of two single prints is required to establish an extreme. The more eager the longer term trader is in this price competition, the more single prints appear and the longer the single print extreme.
quotes: A minimum of two single prints is required to establish an extreme. The more eager the longer term trader is in this price competition, the more the single prints and the longer the single print extreme.

concepts.title: Single Print Local Top or Bottom — Lack of Competition
concepts.body: A local top or bottom is formed when only one single print defines the top or bottom of the range. This condition implies that the market offered a price opportunity which no one really wanted, indicating no evidence of competition. Anything less than two prints suggests that the longer term trader is not very interested in competing at that price.
quotes: Anything less than two prints suggests that the longer term trader is not very interested in competing at that price. A local top or bottom is formed when only one single print defines the top or bottom of the range. This condition implies that the market offered a price opportunity which no one really wanted (i.e., no evidence of competition).

concepts.title: Range Extension as Longer Term Trader Signal
concepts.body: Range extension occurs when longer term traders upset the initial balance by pushing prices beyond the range established in the first two periods. Spirited range extension in multiple periods (e.g., periods C, H, and I) reveals active competition between short term and longer term traders. Range extension is the second clearest indication of longer term trader influence after extremes.
quotes: Range Extension: occurs when longer term traders upset the initial balance.

concepts.title: TPO Buying and Selling Count for Value Area Assessment
concepts.body: TPO (Time Price Opportunity) buying and selling counts within the value area are used to assess whether longer term buyers or sellers control the current session. An imbalance in TPO counts signals directional bias; for example, a TPO buying count of 100 versus a selling count of 59 implies imbalance to the buy side, suggesting prices need to go higher for market balance.
quotes: Imbalance to the buy side implies that prices need to go higher for market balance.

concepts.title: Modal or Fairest Price
concepts.body: The modal or fairest price represents the price level with the most TPO activity within the value area, indicating where the market has spent the most time and considers price most fair. It serves as a reference point for assessing value area buying and selling imbalances.

concepts.title: Hierarchy of Longer Term Trader Activity Signals
concepts.body: Activity at the extremes provides the clearest indication of longer term trader influence, followed by range extension, and then value area buying and selling. Traders should prioritize these signals in this order when assessing directional control.
quotes: Activity at the extremes provides the clearest indication of longer term trader influence, followed by range extension and then value area buying and selling.

concepts.title: Range Extension as Longer Term Trader Signal
concepts.body: Range extension occurs when longer term traders enter the market with enough volume to tip the initial balance and extend the range up or down. Range extension up indicates longer term buying while range extension down indicates longer term selling. On some occasions both longer term buyers and sellers are active at a range extreme but not at the same price and time, creating a situation where both kinds of activity occur in the same price area at different times.
quotes: Range extension up indicates longer term buying while range extension down indicates longer term selling.

concepts.title: Value Area Calculation Using TPOs
concepts.body: The Value Area is determined each trading session by price rotations around the modal price (the price with the highest TPO count or the fairest price). It is computed by counting 70% of all TPOs surrounding the fairest price, representing an estimate of fair value approximated by one standard deviation of the session's trading volume. When a longer term trader makes a trade in the value area, he is buying low or selling high in relation to a longer term view, not in relation to today's value, creating an imbalance in today's value area.
quotes: The value area is an estimate of fair value which is approximated by one standard deviation of the session's trading volume.

concepts.title: TPO Imbalance Detection Procedure
concepts.body: To determine which side contains the longer term imbalance: 1) draw a line through the fairest price, and 2) count TPOs on either side of the fairest price until a single print is encountered. The imbalance is assigned to the side with the smaller number of TPOs because longer term trader activity represents the smaller percentage of total trade in the value area. For example, if the TPO count was 22 above and 12 below the fairest price, that would indicate net TPO selling with a mild bias toward lower prices.
quotes: The imbalance is assigned to the side with the smaller number of TPOs because the longer term trader activity represents the smaller percentage of total trade in the value area.

concepts.title: TPO Analysis Not Applicable on Trend Days
concepts.body: TPO buying and selling analysis in the value area is not applicable on trend days, as the market is still in search of a fair value area. This is an important exception to the standard TPO imbalance detection procedure.
quotes: TPO buying and selling in the value area is not applicable on trend days, as the market is still in search of a fair value area.

concepts.title: Value Area Comparison for Trend Identification
concepts.body: By comparing consecutive days' value areas, traders can identify trend direction and continuation. If today's value area is higher than yesterday's value area, the current market price trend is up. If tomorrow's value area is higher than today's, the current market uptrend has continued. This method offers an alternative to traditional trendline analysis.
quotes: If today's value area is higher than yesterday's value area, then the current market price trend is up.

concepts.title: Cumulative Profile Graphic for Longer Term Balance Assessment
concepts.body: By combining daily consecutive profile graphics into a larger cumulative profile graphic, an evolving picture of longer term balance or imbalance emerges. Individual sessions may suggest an uptrending market without a hint of reversal, but when combined into a cumulative profile, a balanced picture may emerge. Once balanced, a market moves to a state of imbalance which more often than not begins after a final test at the fairest price.
quotes: Once balanced, a market moves to a state of imbalance which, more often than not, begins after a final test at the fairest price.

concepts.title: Market Profile Symmetry and Balance/Imbalance
concepts.body: The symmetry attribute of the profile graphic allows the trader to assess the market's state of balance or imbalance in any timeframe. When a market is symmetric, a condition of balance or equilibrium exists between buyers and sellers. A market imbalance implies price trend continuation as the market works toward a new equilibrium, while market balance is fleeting and implies a directional move is likely to occur, signaling traders to consider trend following methodologies.
quotes: A market imbalance implies Price trend continuation, as the market works toward a new equilibrium.
Market balance, however, is fleeting and implies market change or a directional move (either up or down) is likely to occur.

concepts.title: Profile Graphic for Identifying Support and Resistance
concepts.body: Every trend change occurs at a single moment in time, not conveniently at the end of the hour, day, week or month. The profile graphic can be used to more accurately identify that specific time where control changed hands between buyers and sellers. By pinning down such control shifts, the profile graphic allows the trader to identify key support and resistance levels.
quotes: Every trend change occurs at a single moment in time, not conveniently at the end of the hour, day, week or month.

concepts.title: Goal of Trading System Development
concepts.body: The goal of trading system development is not to develop a system that achieves the highest returns using historical data, but to formulate a sound concept that has performed reasonably well in the past and can be expected to continue to perform reasonably well in the future. The ideal approach is 100% mechanical, increasing the odds that past performance can be replicated in the future.
quotes: Our goal is not to develop a system that achieves the highest returns using historical data, but to formulate a sound concept that has performed reasonably well in the past and can be expected to continue to perform reasonably well in the future.

concepts.title: Benefits of Mechanical Trading Systems
concepts.body: The mechanical approach offers three main benefits: back testing ideas before trading them on historical data rather than hard earned cash; being more objective and less emotional by letting the computer execute trades free of human emotion; and doing more work by covering more markets, trading more systems, and analyzing more time frames each day.
quotes: Analysis (where we have no money at risk) is easy, trading (where we have money at risk) is stress-ful.

concepts.title: 5-Step Plan for Building a Trading System
concepts.body: The five-step plan for building a trading system is: 1) Start with a concept, 2) Turn it into a set of objective rules, 3) Visually check it out on the charts, 4) Formally test it with a computer, 5) Evaluate the results. This structured approach ensures that ideas are properly validated before being traded with real capital.

concepts.title: Developing Trading Concepts Through Chart Study
concepts.body: Traders should develop their own concepts of how markets work by looking at as many charts as possible, trying to identify moving average crossovers, oscillator configurations, price patterns or other pieces of objective evidence which precede major market moves. Traders should also attempt to recognize clues that provide advance warning on moves that are likely to fail. The most profitable ideas are rarely original, but frequently our own.
quotes: The most profitable ideas are rarely original, but frequently our own.

concepts.title: Trend Following vs. Counter Trend Systems
concepts.body: Most successful trading systems are trend following. Counter trend systems should not be overlooked because they bring a degree of negative correlation to the table, meaning that when one system is making money, the other is losing money, resulting in a smoother equity curve for the two systems combined than for either one alone.
quotes: Counter trend systems should not be overlooked, however, because they bring a degree of negative correlation to the table.

concepts.title: Principles of Good Concept Design
concepts.body: Good concepts usually make good sense; if a concept seems to work but makes little sense, it may be coincidence and unlikely to continue working in the future. Concepts must fit the trader's personality to provide discipline during drawdown periods. Concepts should be straightforward and objective, and if trend following, should trade with the major trend, let profits run, and cut losses short. Most importantly, concepts must have a positive expectation.
quotes: Your concepts must fit your personality in order to give you the discipline to follow them even when they are losing money.
Most importantly, your concepts must make money in the long run (i.e. they must have a positive expectation).

concepts.title: Exit Design More Important Than Entry Design
concepts.body: Designing entries is hard, but designing exits is harder and more important. Entry logic is fairly straightforward, but exits have to take various contingencies into account, such as how fast to cut losses or what to do with accumulated profits. Working hard to improve exits will improve returns relative to risk.
quotes: Designing entries is hard, but designing exits is harder and more important.
Work hard to improve your exits, and your returns will improve relative to your risk.

concepts.title: Preference for Non-Reversing Systems
concepts.body: Systems that do not reverse automatically are preferred; it is better to exit a trade first before putting on another trade in the opposite direction. This approach provides more control over position management and avoids the risks of automatic reversal systems.
quotes: I prefer systems that do not reverse automatically—I like to exit a trade first, before putting on another trade in the opposite direction.

concepts.title: Minimize Optimization to Avoid Curve Fitting
concepts.body: Traders should try to optimize as little as possible, as optimization using historical data often leads to expectations of unrealistic returns that cannot be replicated in real trading. Using few parameters and applying the same technique across a number of different markets will improve chances of long run success by reducing the pitfalls of over-optimization.
